summary: Proposed an unseen v6 RAG evaluation cohort focused on title-fragment generalization, paraphrase invariance, abstention under unsupported fragments, and evidence-stability properties without calling paid APIs.

outputs.description: proposal-only worker output contract for the v6 unseen title-fragment and metamorphic/property evaluation cohort
optional human-readable evidence and merge notes for the v6 proposal

selection_policy.select_from_corpus_by_deterministic_hash: Use a fixed seed and record title/document ids after selection; do not inspect generated answers before freezing.
selection_policy.minimum_title_fragment_overlap: Each non-negative case should use 2-5 contiguous title tokens or a noisy abbreviation from a real unseen project title, but ask for a bounded business fact that requires retrieved body evidence.
selection_policy.metamorphic_grouping: At least four cases should be paired or grouped so equivalent or property-preserving variants can be compared for answer and citation stability.

proposed_cases.intent: Given only a short unseen title fragment plus one organization hint, recover the correct project and summarize documented scope without over-reading the title.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: Answer should identify uncertainty if the fragment is insufficient, use retrieved evidence from one matching project, separate documented scope from plausible title inference, and avoid importing similar projects.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: This tests whether retrieval and generation can generalize from partial title evidence to body-grounded scope rather than simply answering another normal full-title question.

proposed_cases.intent: First member of a metamorphic pair: ask about an unseen project using the official title fragment and a narrow deliverable request.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: Return the same core deliverables and citations as qv6_003, with no unsupported expansion beyond the retrieved evidence.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: The score should compare invariance across paired prompts, not just single-case correctness.

proposed_cases.intent: Second member of the pair: ask the same thing as qv6_002 using colloquial synonyms, dropped particles, and a rearranged title fragment.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: Retrieve the same project as qv6_002, preserve the same factual answer, and tolerate noisy wording without changing citations or adding facts.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: The value comes from the metamorphic property that semantically equivalent fragment prompts should yield equivalent grounded answers.

proposed_cases.intent: Provide a generic title fragment shared by multiple unseen RFPs and ask for a specific final procurement result that is not present in RFP evidence.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: Abstain from final vendor, final contract amount, private contact, or award result claims; mention that the available evidence is RFP/planning material only if supported.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: This is a property test for non-identifiability and unsupported-result abstention under ambiguous title fragments.

proposed_cases.intent: Ask about a real unseen title fragment but add a leading assumption from a different domain, such as treating an operation/support project as a new-build AI system.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: Correct the premise using retrieved evidence, keep the answer inside the matched project, and explicitly decline the injected unsupported domain assumption.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: It tests robustness to adversarial premise injection while the title fragment remains partially useful.

proposed_cases.intent: Use two short fragments from different parts of one unseen title and ask the system to split business goals, system functions, and excluded/non-evidenced items.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: Correctly bind both fragments to one project, distinguish goals from functions, and label absent claims as not found rather than filling from title semantics.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: It probes compositional title-fragment binding and claim typing, not another broad summary.

proposed_cases.intent: Repeat an unseen title-fragment query with the same key tokens in reversed or shuffled order.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: Retrieve the same project and produce materially equivalent answer and citations compared with the canonical-order variant selected by the merge owner.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: The evaluated property is order-invariance for title fragments, which exposes brittle lexical matching.

proposed_cases.intent: Use a title fragment that matches at least two unseen near-duplicate projects and add one body-only clue that should disambiguate the correct one.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: Prefer the project supported by the body-only clue, avoid mixing requirements from the distractor title, and state the distinguishing evidence.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: This isolates title-fragment false positives and tests whether body evidence can override superficial title similarity.

proposed_cases.intent: Ask a normal unseen fragment question, then inject an irrelevant but plausible extra sentence naming a different organization or technology.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: The answer should remain stable against the injected sentence unless retrieved evidence supports it; citations should stay on the original matching project.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: This is a metamorphic irrelevant-input test: adding irrelevant text should not change the grounded answer.

proposed_cases.intent: Run a controlled variant where the only supporting chunk for an unseen fragment-specific claim is withheld or marked unavailable by the harness.
proposed_cases.expected_behavior: The system should abstain or say the documents do not contain enough evidence, instead of reconstructing the answer from the title fragment.
proposed_cases.why_not_just_question_diversification: This directly tests the evidence-removal property recommended in the R&D note and catches title-only hallucination.

freeze_and_exposure_notes: Freeze the concrete instantiated questions before any scored run.
Record selection seed, selected document ids, and title fragments in a manifest.
After answer inspection, mark the v6 cohort as first-run-only or regression-only in the exposure registry.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Reusing v4/v5 exposed organizations or exact failure prompts
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: Would make v6 another regression set rather than unseen validation.
Metamorphic failures can hide behind high single-answer groundedness and relevance scores.

needs_orchestrator_review: Instantiate concrete Korean prompts from unseen corpus titles using deterministic selection and freeze before execution.
Decide whether qv6_010 evidence-removal should be executed in v6 or deferred to v7 if harness support is not yet available.
Add pair/group-aware scoring so qv6_002/qv6_003 and order/irrelevant-context variants are compared against each other, not only judged independently.

observed_results: Protected source, script, log, task, and existing question-set paths were not modified.
v4/v5 exposed IDs and failure themes were reviewed to avoid direct reuse.
Proposal contains 10 cases, within requested 8-12 range.
